title: HK Fire Report: Contractor Violations and Regulatory Collapse
summary: A 627-page investigation report on the 2025 Hong Kong fire that killed 168 people revealed that the contractor deliberately used non-flame retardant materials and submitted fraudulent fire certificates, while multiple government agencies failed to enforce safety regulations. This disaster exposes systemic failures in Hong Kong&\#x27;s building maintenance oversight, highlighting the deadly consequences of self-regulation and inadequate enforcement. The findings could drive reforms in construction safety and regulatory accountability across the region. The contractor Hung Yip Construction used non-flame retardant safety nets and foam boards on a facade renovation, and tampered with fire safety systems including draining water tanks and shutting off electrical switches. The Housing Bureau&\#x27;s independent review team conducted only file checks and gave advance notice of inspections.
context: During high-rise building renovations, construction safety nets and foam insulation are often used. Flame retardant materials are critical to prevent rapid fire spread, especially in vertical shafts like stairwells. The fireman&\#x27;s switch and fire sprinkler systems must remain operational during construction to ensure fire safety.
